Appearances
Ethan A. Wimert, Esq., HEINRICH LAW, PC (510) 944-0110 ethan@heinrichlaw.net Attorneys for Plaintiff CAROL BARON (via Zoom Conference)
MILES F. MAURINO, Esq., HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG, LLP, 415.766.3200 mmaurino@hpylaw.com Attorney for Defendants J&A SEVEN WONDERS CORP. (A DISSOLVED CORPORATION) & GROCERY OUTLET, INC. (Personal appearance)

Not ARUGED. Discussion regarding trial continuance is held. Court informs counsel that trial continuances are heard in Department 206. Department 302 does not hear matters re: trial continuanes.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CAROL BARON Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) If trial continuance is thought necessary, motion may be made to Department 206. Then, if more time is thought necessary for a summary judgment motion, motion may be made to this department. "A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend." (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
